hotels.name: Modern 1–3 Bedroom Short-Stay Apartments in Tema - Nightly Stay
hotels.description: Experience comfort, convenience, and modern living in our beautifully furnished 1-, 2-, and 3-bedroom short-stay apartments located in the heart of Tema. Designed to suit solo travellers, couples, families, business teams, and small groups, each unit offers a warm and welcoming atmosphere with spacious layouts and contemporary finishing. All apartments feature a cozy living and dining area, fully equipped kitchen with modern appliances, air-conditioning, high-speed Wi-Fi, smart TV, and comfortable bedrooms with quality bedding. The 2- and 3-bedroom units offer additional space and privacy, with extra bathrooms and larger living zones ideal for families or group stays. Situated in a secure and serene neighbourhood in Tema, the property offers 24/7 security, free parking, reliable water supply, and close access to major business districts, restaurants, shopping centres, the port area, and the coastal stretch. Whether you are staying short-term or for an extended period, you’ll enjoy a perfect blend of convenience, style, and homelike comfort.

tours.name: Boti Falls
tours.description: Boti Falls, nestled in the Eastern Region of Ghana, is a captivating natural attraction famous for its enchanting twin waterfalls affectionately referred to as "male" and "female" falls. Surrounded by lush forest, mystical rock formations, and hidden caves, Boti Falls offers visitors a refreshing escape into nature with opportunities for hiking, swimming, sightseeing, and cultural discovery.

tours.name: Cape Coast Castle
tours.description: Step inside one of Ghana’s most powerful historical landmarks. Explore Cape Coast Castle’s dungeons, governor’s quarters, and the Door of No Return. Learn about Ghana’s role in the trans-Atlantic slave trade through guided storytelling and preserved artifacts — a moving experience that connects past and present.

tours.name: Kakum National Park – Canopy Walk, Hikes
tours.description: Experience Ghana’s lush rainforest above on Kakum’s famous Canopy Walkway, suspended 30 meters high among the treetops. Enjoy guided nature hikes, spot rare wildlife, and immerse yourself in the park’s rich biodiversity and serene forest atmosphere.

tours.name: Nzulezo Stilt Village Tour
tours.description: Glide through the serene waters of Lake Tadane on a guided canoe tour to Nzulezo — a breathtaking village built entirely on stilts. Discover the unique way of life of the people who live above water, rich cultural traditions, and stunning scenery in one of Ghana’s most remarkable communities - Canoe ride, Nzulezo village walking tour, storytelling (History of Nzulezo), Birdwatch.
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Aburi Botanical Gardens
tours.description: Aburi Botanical Gardens is a legacy of colonial-era science, ecology, and botanical innovation. Established in 1890 after several aborted attempts, it transformed a sanatorium site into West Africa’s most important living laboratory. Today, it balances conservation, tourism, education, and research perfect for nature lovers, historians, and scientists alike. Visitors meander along palm-lined lanes, discover botanical marvels from orchid beds to centuries-old ficus trees and explore whimsical features like a retired helicopter or themed gardens. A calm picnic spot, a flower-filled walkway, and guided interpretation enrich the experience. A visit here is not just scenic; it is educational, restorative, and timeless.
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Bonwire Kente Weaving Village
tours.description: Visitors to Bonwire can explore local weaving centers, learn about the origins and meanings of Kente patterns, watch skilled artisans at work, and even try weaving their own pieces. The experience is both educational and hands-on, making it perfect for students, tourists, and culture lovers.

tours.name: Mole National Park
tours.description: Mole National Park, located in northern Ghana’s Savannah Region, is the country’s largest and most iconic wildlife reserve. Established in the 1950s and gazetted in 1971, it is home to elephants, antelopes, baboons, buffalo, and hundreds of bird species. Visitors can enjoy walking and jeep safaris, affordable tours, and breathtaking views from eco-lodges like Mole Motel and Zaina Lodge. With its rich biodiversity and eco-tourism experiences, Mole remains a top travel destination in Ghana.
locations: northern Ghana’s Savannah Region, is the country’s largest a
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Wli Agumatsa Waterfalls
tours.description: Wli Agumatsa Waterfalls is a breathtaking natural wonder and the highest waterfall in West Africa. As you leave the bustle behind and walk through the Agumatsa Wildlife Sanctuary, you’re surrounded by towering trees, fluttering butterflies, the chatter of birds, and the sudden roar of water cascading eighty metres down rock faces.

tours.name: Shai Hills Resource Reserve
tours.description: Shai Hills Resource Reserve is a surprise of rocky outcrops, ancient caves and wide savanna that shelters baboons, antelopes, birds and cultural traces of the Shai people.
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Kwame Nkrumah Mausoleum
tours.description: Discover the life and legacy of Ghana’s founding President in the beautifully renovated Memorial Park, located on the historic grounds where Ghana’s independence was declared. Walk through lush grounds, pay respects at the marble mausoleum, explore personal relics in the museum, and enjoy modern amenities like a presidential library, amphitheatre, and a cultural restaurant.
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Bojo Beach
tours.description: Bojo Beach offers a tranquil escape from Accra’s bustle, great for those seeking peace, sandy shores, and a quiet swim. The boat access and private feel add to its charm. Just come prepared: bring water, sunscreen, and a relaxed mindset, especially on weekends when the beach can get busier and facilities may show strain.
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Larabanga Mosque

tours.name: Wechiau Community Hippo Sanctuary
tours.description: Nestled along the Black Volta River in Ghana’s Upper West Region, the Wechiau Community Hippo Sanctuary (WCHS) is a living example of how communities, tradition, and conservation can coexist. This sanctuary protects one of only two remaining hippo populations in the country, preserves riverine forests and floodplain habitats, and supports livelihoods for 20 neighboring communities. From river safaris to village culture, bird-watching, and community-led conservation, Wechiau offers a wild experience wrapped in local warmth and sustainable tourism.
locations: Volta River in Ghana’s Upper West Region, the Wechiau Commun
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Tafi-Atome Monkey Sanctuary & Cultural Village
nearby: 24 hours in advance for a full refund.

tours.name: Assin Manso Slave River Site
tours.description: Step into the echoes of history at the Assin Manso Slave River Site; a sacred and emotional landmark that tells the untold stories of enslaved Africans. Located in Ghana’s Central Region, this site marks the final place where enslaved people bathed before being marched to coastal castles like Cape Coast and Elmina, never to see home again. Visitors today can walk the path of their ancestors, reflect at the serene Slave River (“Donkor Nsuo”), explore the ancestral burial site of two repatriated Africans, and learn from experienced guides about Ghana’s role in the transatlantic slave trade. The site is not just a monument to pain, but a space of remembrance, healing, and reconnection for Ghanaians and members of the African diaspora. Whether you're a history enthusiast, heritage traveler, student, or educator, a visit to Assin Manso offers a deeply moving and educational experience.
